title: Sizes of Things on my Website Tour - Stoned

enrichment.summary: Kyle gives a stoned tour of his personal 'sizes of things' website, which maps 45 size dimensions from the electron to the known universe, noting humans sit at dimension 19 — 58% closer in size to the electron than to the universe. He walks through each scale level describing what exists at each order of magnitude, from subatomic particles up through galaxies and superclusters, then plays a comparison game matching small things to large analogs across the scale.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.topic: human position in the scale of things
stances.position: Finds it remarkable and trippy that we sit nearly in the middle of all known size scales
Enjoys and recommends it; notes he is sobering up and needs to get high again before finishing
Amused that the first Death Star was relatively small compared to the moon, and the second was much bigger

claims.subject: size dimensions
claims.claim: There are 45 size dimensions ranging from the electron to the known universe.

claims.subject: human size
claims.claim: Humans are remarkably close to the middle of the size dimensions, specifically at size dimension number 19.

claims.subject: human size
claims.claim: Humans are 58% closer in size to the electron than to the universe out of the 45 size dimensions.

claims.subject: size dimensions
claims.claim: Without the anchor dimension of one meter, there are 18 smaller dimensions and 26 larger dimensions.

claims.subject: electron
claims.claim: The electron is the smallest thing we know, with a size of one quintillionth of a meter, also known as an attometer.

claims.subject: size dimensions
claims.claim: Each size dimension is 10 times smaller or larger than the adjacent one.

claims.subject: weak force
claims.claim: The weak force range is how close subatomic particles need to be to have a gravitational effect on each other.

claims.subject: helium nucleus
claims.claim: The helium nucleus is visible at size dimension negative 14, along with neutrons and protons.

claims.subject: gold nucleus
claims.claim: The gold nucleus and uranium nucleus are visible at size dimension negative 12.

claims.subject: atom structure
claims.claim: Most of an atom is empty space; if the nucleus were the size of a tennis ball, the electron would orbit at the edge of a football stadium.

claims.subject: hydrogen atom
claims.claim: The hydrogen atom is the smallest atom, visible at size dimension negative 10.

claims.subject: DNA
claims.claim: The DNA double helix is visible at size dimension negative 8, along with proteins and bucky balls.

claims.subject: bucky balls
claims.claim: Bucky balls, made of 60 carbon atoms, can exhibit wave-like behavior in the double-slit experiment.

claims.subject: viruses
claims.claim: The polio virus and herpes virus are visible at size dimension negative 7, alongside other viruses and nanotechnology.

claims.subject: visible light
claims.claim: Visible light wavelengths are about the same size as red blood cells and olfactory receptors.

claims.subject: radio waves
claims.claim: Radio waves are about the same size as green light wavelengths.

claims.subject: fruit fly
claims.claim: A fruit fly is visible at size dimension negative 4, alongside crystals and pollen.

claims.subject: human scale
claims.claim: Humans live at size dimension 0, which is one meter across, encompassing everyday objects like basketballs and hands.

claims.subject: large animals
claims.claim: The largest animals, like horses and dragons, are visible at size dimension positive 1.

claims.subject: buildings
claims.claim: Buildings and the largest trees are visible at size dimension positive 2.

claims.subject: asteroids
claims.claim: An asteroid predicted to hit in 2029 is visible at size dimension positive 3.

claims.subject: Mount Everest
claims.claim: Mount Everest's height is visible at size dimension positive 5 (100 kilometers).

claims.subject: Death Star
claims.claim: The Death Star from Star Wars is comparable in size to the moon at size dimension positive 7.

claims.subject: Earth
claims.claim: The Earth is visible at size dimension positive 8, alongside other planets like Saturn.

claims.subject: Sun
claims.claim: The Sun is visible at size dimension positive 10, alongside other stars like Sirius.

claims.subject: solar system
claims.claim: The solar system is visible at size dimension positive 12, spanning about half a billion kilometers.

claims.subject: nebulae
claims.claim: Planetary nebulae are visible at size dimension positive 14, comparable in size to the solar system.

claims.subject: Milky Way
claims.claim: The Milky Way galaxy is visible at size dimension positive 21.

claims.subject: superclusters
claims.claim: Galaxy superclusters are visible at size dimension positive 24.

claims.subject: universe
claims.claim: The known universe is the largest thing we know, encompassing all size dimensions up to positive 26.

claims.subject: size comparison
claims.claim: A brain cell is to a human what a human is to a skyscraper.

claims.subject: size comparison
claims.claim: A red blood cell is to a human what a human is to a city like Denver.

claims.subject: size comparison
claims.claim: A protein is to a human what a human is to the Earth.

claims.subject: size comparison
claims.claim: An atom is to a human what a human is to the solar system.

claims.subject: size comparison
claims.claim: An electron is to a human what a human is to a nebula.
